name: avro-python3

metadata.name: avro-python3
classifiers: License :: OSI Approved :: Apache Software License
metadata.summary: Avro is a serialization and RPC framework.

documentation:
# Deprecation Notice

[avro-python3](https://pypi.org/project/avro-python3/) is no longer maintained and is deprecated in favor of [avro](https://pypi.org/project/avro/). Avro is maintained and has full support for recent versions of Python.

## Apache Avro™

Apache Avro™ is a data serialization system. To learn more, please visit our [website](https://avro.apache.org/).


### Documentation

Apache Avro documentation is maintained on our [wiki](https://cwiki.apache.org/confluence/display/AVRO/Index).


### Contributing

To contribute to Avro, please read [How to Contribute](https://cwiki.apache.org/confluence/display/AVRO/How+To+Contribute) on the wiki.


### License, Credits and Acknowledgements

License, credits and acknowledgements are maintained in the [LICENSE.txt](https://github.com/apache/avro/blob/master/LICENSE.txt) and [NOTICE.txt](https://github.com/apache/avro/blob/master/NOTICE.txt) in the source code repository. Those files are also included with the installed package.

functions.name: avro.schemanormalization.ToParsingCanonicalForm
documentation:
Returns the "Parsing Canonical Form" of a schema.

The Parsing Canonical Form is defined by the Avro specification.

Args:
    schema: The Schema to be normalized.
Returns:
    A string containing the canonical JSON schema.

functions.name: avro.schemanormalization.Fingerprint
documentation:
Returns a fingerprint of a string of bytes.

Args:
  parsing_normal_form_schema: A string containing an Avro
    schema in parsing normal form, such as one obtained
    by passing a schema object to ToParsingCanonicalForm()
  fingerprint_algorithm_name: One of the algorithm names
    returned by FingerprintAlgorithmNames(), typically
    'CRC-64-AVRO', 'md5' or 'sha256'. See the Avro
    specification for guidance on selecting a
    fingerprinting algorithm.
Returns:
    A bytes object containing the schema fingerprint.

functions.name: avro.schemanormalization.FingerprintAlgorithmNames
documentation:
A collection of fingerprint algorithm names.

The same algorithm may be associated with more than one entry
in this collection. For example, 'SHA-1' and 'sha1' might both
be present in the result, in order to facilitate the interoperability
of algorithm names between Python and, say, Java Avro
implementations.

Returns:
  A set of strings containing algorithm names, any
  one of which can be used as the fingerprint_algorithm
  argument of Fingerprint()

functions.name: avro.schemanormalization._Crc64AvroFingerprint
documentation:
The 64-bit Rabin Fingerprint.

As described in the Avro specification.

Args:
  data: A bytes object containing the UTF-8 encoded parsing canonical
    form of an Avro schema.
Returns:
  A bytes object with a length of eight.

modules.name: setup
documentation:
Provide the code necessary for packaging and installing avro-python3.

The avro-python3 software is designed for Python 3, but this file and the packaging software supports Python 2.7.

functions.name: avro.protocol.ProtocolFromJSONData
documentation:
Builds an Avro  Protocol from its JSON descriptor.

Args:

Returns:
  The Avro Protocol parsed from the JSON descriptor.
Raises:
  ProtocolParseException: if the descriptor is invalid.

documentation:
Constructs a Protocol from its JSON descriptor in text form.

Args:
  json_string: String representation of the JSON descriptor of the protocol.
Returns:
  The parsed protocol.
Raises:
  ProtocolParseException: on JSON parsing error,
      or if the JSON descriptor is invalid.

classes.name: avro.protocol.Message

methods.name: avro.protocol.Message._ParseRequestFromJSONDesc
documentation:
Parses the request descriptor of a protocol message.

Args:
  request_desc: Descriptor of the message request.
      This is a list of fields that defines an unnamed record.
  names: Tracker for named Avro schemas.
Returns:
  The parsed request schema, as an unnamed record.

methods.name: avro.protocol.Message._ParseResponseFromJSONDesc
documentation:
Parses the response descriptor of a protocol message.

Args:
  response_desc: Descriptor of the message response.
      This is an arbitrary Avro schema descriptor.
Returns:
  The parsed response schema.

methods.name: avro.protocol.Message._ParseErrorsFromJSONDesc
documentation:
Parses the errors descriptor of a protocol message.

Args:
  errors_desc: Descriptor of the errors thrown by the protocol message.
      This is a list of error types understood as an implicit union.
      Each error type is an arbitrary Avro schema.
  names: Tracker for named Avro schemas.
Returns:
  The parsed ErrorUnionSchema.
A Protocol message.

methods.name: avro.ipc.BaseRequestor.Request

Writes a request message and reads a response or error message.

  request_datum: IPC request.
Returns:

methods.name: avro.ipc.BaseRequestor._WriteHandshakeRequest
documentation:
Emits the handshake request.

Args:
  encoder: Encoder to write the handshake request into.

methods.name: avro.ipc.BaseRequestor._WriteCallRequest
documentation:
The format of a call request is:
  * request metadata, a map with values of type bytes
  * the message name, an Avro string, followed by
  * the message parameters. Parameters are serialized according to
    the message's request declaration.

methods.name: avro.ipc.BaseRequestor._ReadHandshakeResponse
documentation:
Reads and processes the handshake response message.

Args:
  decoder: Decoder to read messages from.
Returns:
  call-response exists (boolean) ???
Raises:
  schema.AvroException on ???

methods.name: avro.ipc.BaseRequestor._ReadCallResponse
documentation:
Reads and processes a method call response.

The format of a call response is:
  - response metadata, a map with values of type bytes
  - a one-byte error flag boolean, followed by either:
    - if the error flag is false,
      the message response, serialized per the message's response schema.
    - if the error flag is true,
      the error, serialized per the message's error union schema.

Args:
  message_name:
  decoder:
Returns:
  ???
Raises:
  schema.AvroException on ???
Base class for the client side of a protocol interaction.
Base IPC Classes (Requestor/Responder)

modules.name: avro.io
documentation:
Input/output utilities.

Includes:
 - i/o-specific constants
 - i/o-specific exceptions
 - schema validation
 - leaf value encoding and decoding
 - datum reader/writer stuff (?)

Also includes a generic representation for data, which uses the
following mapping:
 - Schema records are implemented as dict.
 - Schema arrays are implemented as list.
 - Schema maps are implemented as dict.
 - Schema strings are implemented as unicode.
 - Schema bytes are implemented as str.
 - Schema ints are implemented as int.
 - Schema longs are implemented as long.
 - Schema floats are implemented as float.
 - Schema doubles are implemented as float.
 - Schema booleans are implemented as bool.
!/usr/bin/env python3
-*- mode: python -*-
-*- coding: utf-8 -*-
Licensed to the Apache Software Foundation (ASF) under one
or more contributor license agreements.  See the NOTICE file
distributed with this work for additional information
regarding copyright ownership.  The ASF licenses this file
to you under the Apache License, Version 2.0 (the
"License"); you may not use this file except in compliance
with the License.  You may obtain a copy of the License at
https://www.apache.org/licenses/LICENSE-2.0
Unless required by applicable law or agreed to in writing, software
distributed under the License is distributed on an "AS IS" BASIS,
WITHOUT WARRANTIES OR CONDITIONS OF ANY KIND, either express or implied.
See the License for the specific language governing permissions and
limitations under the License.

documentation:
Determines if a python datum is an instance of a schema.

Args:

  datum: Datum to validate.
Returns:

documentation: Raised when datum is not an example of schema.

classes.name: avro.io.BinaryDecoder

methods.name: avro.io.BinaryDecoder.__init__
documentation: reader is a Python object on which we can call read, seek, and tell.

methods.name: avro.io.BinaryDecoder.reader
documentation: Reports the reader used by this decoder.

methods.name: avro.io.BinaryDecoder.read
documentation:
Read n bytes.

Args:
  n: Number of bytes to read.
Returns:
  The next n bytes from the input.

methods.name: avro.io.BinaryDecoder.read_null
documentation: null is written as zero bytes

methods.name: avro.io.BinaryDecoder.read_boolean
documentation:
a boolean is written as a single byte
whose value is either 0 (false) or 1 (true).

methods.name: avro.io.BinaryDecoder.read_int
documentation: int and long values are written using variable-length, zig-zag coding.

methods.name: avro.io.BinaryDecoder.read_long
documentation: int and long values are written using variable-length, zig-zag coding.

methods.name: avro.io.BinaryDecoder.read_float
documentation:
A float is written as 4 bytes.
The float is converted into a 32-bit integer using a method equivalent to
Java's floatToIntBits and then encoded in little-endian format.

methods.name: avro.io.BinaryDecoder.read_double
documentation:
A double is written as 8 bytes.
The double is converted into a 64-bit integer using a method equivalent to
Java's doubleToLongBits and then encoded in little-endian format.

methods.name: avro.io.BinaryDecoder.read_bytes
documentation: Bytes are encoded as a long followed by that many bytes of data.

methods.name: avro.io.BinaryDecoder.read_utf8
documentation:
A string is encoded as a long followed by
that many bytes of UTF-8 encoded character data.
Read leaf values.
Decoder/Encoder

A float is written as 4 bytes.
The float is converted into a 32-bit integer using a method equivalent to

A double is written as 8 bytes.
The double is converted into a 64-bit integer using a method equivalent to

classes.name: avro.io.DatumReader

methods.name: avro.io.DatumReader.__init__
documentation:
As defined in the Avro specification, we call the schema encoded
in the data the "writer's schema", and the schema expected by the
reader the "reader's schema".

methods.name: avro.io.DatumReader.set_writer_schema
documentation: read/write properties

methods.name: avro.io.DatumReader.read_fixed
documentation:
Fixed instances are encoded using the number of bytes declared
in the schema.

methods.name: avro.io.DatumReader.read_enum
documentation:
An enum is encoded by a int, representing the zero-based position
of the symbol in the schema.

methods.name: avro.io.DatumReader.read_array
documentation:
Arrays are encoded as a series of blocks.

Each block consists of a long count value,
followed by that many array items.
A block with count zero indicates the end of the array.
Each item is encoded per the array's item schema.

If a block's count is negative,
then the count is followed immediately by a long block size,
indicating the number of bytes in the block.
The actual count in this case
is the absolute value of the count written.

methods.name: avro.io.DatumReader.read_map
documentation:
Maps are encoded as a series of blocks.

Each block consists of a long count value,
followed by that many key/value pairs.
A block with count zero indicates the end of the map.
Each item is encoded per the map's value schema.

If a block's count is negative,
then the count is followed immediately by a long block size,
indicating the number of bytes in the block.
The actual count in this case
is the absolute value of the count written.

methods.name: avro.io.DatumReader.read_union
documentation:
A union is encoded by first writing an int value indicating
the zero-based position within the union of the schema of its value.
The value is then encoded per the indicated schema within the union.

methods.name: avro.io.DatumReader.read_record
documentation:
A record is encoded by encoding the values of its fields
in the order that they are declared. In other words, a record
is encoded as just the concatenation of the encodings of its fields.
Field values are encoded per their schema.

Schema Resolution:
 * the ordering of fields may be different: fields are matched by name.
 * schemas for fields with the same name in both records are resolved
   recursively.
 * if the writer's record contains a field with a name not present in the
   reader's record, the writer's value for that field is ignored.
 * if the reader's record schema has a field that contains a default value,
   and writer's schema does not have a field with the same name, then the
   reader should use the default value from its field.
 * if the reader's record schema has a field with no default value, and
   writer's schema does not have a field with the same name, then the
   field's value is unset.

methods.name: avro.io.DatumReader._read_default_value
documentation: Basically a JSON Decoder?
Deserialize Avro-encoded data into a Python data structure.
DatumReader/Writer

classes.name: avro.io.DatumWriter

documentation:
An enum is encoded by a int, representing the zero-based position

methods.name: avro.io.DatumWriter.write_array
documentation:
Arrays are encoded as a series of blocks.

Each block consists of a long count value,
followed by that many array items.
A block with count zero indicates the end of the array.
Each item is encoded per the array's item schema.

If a block's count is negative,
then the count is followed immediately by a long block size,
indicating the number of bytes in the block.
The actual count in this case
is the absolute value of the count written.

methods.name: avro.io.DatumWriter.write_map
documentation:
Maps are encoded as a series of blocks.

Each block consists of a long count value,
followed by that many key/value pairs.
A block with count zero indicates the end of the map.
Each item is encoded per the map's value schema.

If a block's count is negative,
then the count is followed immediately by a long block size,
indicating the number of bytes in the block.
The actual count in this case
is the absolute value of the count written.

methods.name: avro.io.DatumWriter.write_union
documentation:
A union is encoded by first writing an int value indicating
the zero-based position within the union of the schema of its value.
The value is then encoded per the indicated schema within the union.

methods.name: avro.io.DatumWriter.write_record
documentation:
A record is encoded by encoding the values of its fields
in the order that they are declared. In other words, a record
is encoded as just the concatenation of the encodings of its fields.
Field values are encoded per their schema.
DatumWriter for generic python objects.

functions.name: avro.schema.FilterKeysOut
documentation:
Filters a collection of (key, value) items.

Exclude any item whose key belongs to keys.

Args:
  items: Dictionary of items to filter the keys out of.
  keys: Keys to filter out.
Yields:
  Filtered items.
Module functions

functions.name: avro.schema.SchemaFromJSONData
documentation:
Builds an Avro Schema from its JSON descriptor.

Args:
  json_data: JSON data representing the descriptor of the Avro schema.
  names: Optional tracker for Avro named schemas.
Returns:
  The Avro schema parsed from the JSON descriptor.
Raises:
  SchemaParseException: if the descriptor is invalid.

functions.name: avro.schema.parse
documentation:
Constructs a Schema from its JSON descriptor in text form.

Args:
  json_string: String representation of the JSON descriptor of the schema.
Returns:
  The parsed schema.
Raises:
  SchemaParseException: on JSON parsing error,
      or if the JSON descriptor is invalid.

functions.name: avro.schema.Parse
documentation: Deprecated implementation of parse.

classes.name: avro.schema.Error
documentation: Base class for errors in this module.
Exceptions

classes.name: avro.schema.AvroException
documentation: Generic Avro schema error.

classes.name: avro.schema.SchemaParseException
documentation: Error while parsing a JSON schema descriptor.

classes.name: avro.schema.MappingProxyEncoder
documentation: Utilities

classes.name: avro.schema.Schema

methods.name: avro.schema.Schema.__init__
documentation:
Initializes a new schema object.

Args:
  type: Type of the schema to initialize.
  other_props: Optional dictionary of additional properties.

methods.name: avro.schema.Schema.namespace
documentation: Returns: the namespace this schema belongs to, if any, or None.

methods.name: avro.schema.Schema.type
documentation: Returns: the type of this schema.

methods.name: avro.schema.Schema.doc
documentation: Returns: the documentation associated to this schema, if any, or None.

methods.name: avro.schema.Schema.props
documentation:
Reports all the properties of this schema.

Includes all properties, reserved and non reserved.
JSON properties of this schema are directly generated from this dict.

Returns:
  A read-only dictionary of properties associated to this schema.

methods.name: avro.schema.Schema.other_props
documentation: Returns: the dictionary of non-reserved properties.

methods.name: avro.schema.Schema.__str__
documentation: Returns: the JSON representation of this schema.

methods.name: avro.schema.Schema.to_json
documentation:
Converts the schema object into its AVRO specification representation.

Schema types that have names (records, enums, and fixed) must
be aware of not re-defining schemas that are already listed
in the parameter names.
Abstract base class for all Schema classes.

classes.name: avro.schema.Name

methods.name: avro.schema.Name.__init__
documentation:
Parses an Avro name.

Args:
  name: Avro name to parse (relative or absolute).
  namespace: Optional explicit namespace if the name is relative.

methods.name: avro.schema.Name.simple_name
documentation: Returns: the simple name part of this name.

methods.name: avro.schema.Name.namespace
documentation: Returns: this name's namespace, possible the empty string.

methods.name: avro.schema.Name.fullname
documentation: Returns: the full name.
Representation of an Avro name.

classes.name: avro.schema.Names

methods.name: avro.schema.Names.__init__
documentation:
Initializes a new name tracker.

Args:
  default_namespace: Optional default namespace.
  names: Optional initial mapping of known named schemas.

methods.name: avro.schema.Names.names
documentation: Returns: the mapping of known named schemas.

methods.name: avro.schema.Names.default_namespace
documentation: Returns: the default namespace, if any, or None.

methods.name: avro.schema.Names.NewWithDefaultNamespace
documentation:
Creates a new name tracker from this tracker, but with a new default ns.

Args:
  namespace: New default namespace to use.
Returns:
  New name tracker with the specified default namespace.

methods.name: avro.schema.Names.GetName
documentation:
Resolves the Avro name according to this name tracker's state.

Args:
  name: Name to resolve (absolute or relative).
  namespace: Optional explicit namespace.
Returns:
  The specified name, resolved according to this tracker.

methods.name: avro.schema.Names.GetSchema
documentation:
Resolves an Avro schema by name.

Args:
  name: Name (relative or absolute) of the Avro schema to look up.
  namespace: Optional explicit namespace.
Returns:
  The schema with the specified name, if any, or None.

methods.name: avro.schema.Names.prune_namespace
documentation:
given a properties, return properties with namespace removed if
it matches the own default namespace

methods.name: avro.schema.Names.Register
documentation:
Registers a new named schema in this tracker.

Args:
  schema: Named Avro schema to register in this tracker.
Tracks Avro named schemas and default namespace during parsing.

classes.name: avro.schema.NamedSchema

methods.name: avro.schema.NamedSchema.__init__
documentation:
Initializes a new named schema object.

Args:
  type: Type of the named schema.
  name: Name (absolute or relative) of the schema.
  namespace: Optional explicit namespace if name is relative.
  names: Tracker to resolve and register Avro names.
  other_props: Optional map of additional properties of the schema.

methods.name: avro.schema.NamedSchema.avro_name
documentation: Returns: the Name object describing this schema's name.

methods.name: avro.schema.NamedSchema.name_ref
documentation:
Reports this schema name relative to the specified name tracker.

Args:
  names: Avro name tracker to relativise this schema name against.
Returns:
  This schema name, relativised against the specified name tracker.

Abstract base class for named schemas.

Named schemas are enumerated in NAMED_TYPES.

Args:

  has_default:

  order:
  names:

  other_props:

methods.name: avro.schema.Field.type
documentation: Returns: the schema of this field.

methods.name: avro.schema.Field.name
documentation: Returns: this field name.

methods.name: avro.schema.Field.index
documentation: Returns: the 0-based index of this field in the record.
Representation of the schema of a field in a record.

Initializes a new schema object for the specified primitive type.

methods.name: avro.schema.PrimitiveSchema.name
documentation: Returns: the simple name of this schema.

methods.name: avro.schema.PrimitiveSchema.fullname

Schema of a primitive Avro type.

Valid primitive types are defined in PRIMITIVE_TYPES.

Args:

classes.name: avro.schema.ErrorUnionSchema

methods.name: avro.schema.ErrorUnionSchema.__init__

Initializes an error-union schema.

Args:

Schema representing the declared errors of a protocol message.

classes.name: avro.schema.RecordSchema

methods.name: avro.schema.RecordSchema._MakeField
documentation:
Builds field schemas from a list of field JSON descriptors.

Args:
  index: 0-based index of the field in the record.
  field_desc: JSON descriptors of a record field.
  names: Avro schema tracker.
Return:
  The field schema.

methods.name: avro.schema.RecordSchema._MakeFieldList
documentation:
Builds field schemas from a list of field JSON descriptors.

Guarantees field name unicity.

Args:
  field_desc_list: collection of field JSON descriptors.
  names: Avro schema tracker.
Yields
  Field schemas.

methods.name: avro.schema.RecordSchema._MakeFieldMap
documentation:
Builds the field map.

Guarantees field name unicity.

Args:
  fields: iterable of field schema.
Returns:
  A read-only map of field schemas, indexed by name.

methods.name: avro.schema.RecordSchema.__init__
documentation:
Initializes a new record schema object.

Args:
  name: Name of the record (absolute or relative).
  namespace: Optional namespace the record belongs to, if name is relative.
  fields: collection of fields to add to this record.
      Exactly one of fields or make_fields must be specified.
  make_fields: function creating the fields that belong to the record.
      The function signature is: make_fields(names) -> ordered field list.
      Exactly one of fields or make_fields must be specified.
  names:
  record_type: Type of the record: one of RECORD, ERROR or REQUEST.
      Protocol requests are not named.
  doc:
  other_props:

methods.name: avro.schema.RecordSchema.fields
documentation: Returns: the field schemas, as an ordered tuple.

methods.name: avro.schema.RecordSchema.field_map
documentation: Returns: a read-only map of the field schemas index by field names.
Schema of a record.
